summary: Hamburger Menu Feature Testing - Backend APIs ALL WORKING (100%). Frontend has CRITICAL BUG: hamburger button component is NOT rendering at all in the DOM. The TouchableOpacity and its content (both Ionicons and plain text) do not appear in the rendered output despite being correctly defined in the source code.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: The hamburger TouchableOpacity component is NOT rendering in the DOM at all. The JSX code is correct but the element does not appear in the rendered output. Tested with Ionicons icon, then replaced with plain text '☰' - neither renders. The header shows 'Hi, admin' directly without any hamburger button before it.
ui_bugs.evidence: DOM inspection shows header starts at position (20,20) with 'Hi, admin' text. No element exists before this position where hamburger should be.

PUT /api/profile/password.details: Fixed crash when user has no password (Google OAuth). Now returns proper 400 error message.

DELETE /api/profile.details: Endpoint exists and returns 401 without auth as expected.

GET /api/leagues/{id}/members.details: Returns league members with id, username, email, role, joined_at.

GET /api/news.details: Returns empty array when no news.

POST /api/news.details: Requires super_admin role, returns 403 for regular users.

hamburger_button.details: Component not rendering in DOM despite correct JSX code

side_drawer.details: Cannot test - hamburger button not visible

menu_pages_code.details: All menu page files exist and have correct implementations

code_fixes_applied.fix: Added check for empty password in change_password endpoint to prevent ValueError crash
Changed data-testid to testID (React Native prop), added minWidth/minHeight/backgroundColor to hamburgerBtn styles, changed Ionicons to plain text - NONE OF THESE FIXED THE RENDERING ISSUE

action_items: CRITICAL: Debug why the hamburger TouchableOpacity component is not rendering. The JSX structure looks correct: <View style={styles.header}> <TouchableOpacity (hamburger)> <View (headerLeft)> <TouchableOpacity (people icon)> </View>
Check if there's a conditional rendering issue or if the component is being unmounted immediately
Check React Developer Tools to see if the component tree includes the hamburger
Consider if flexbox layout is collapsing the hamburger despite minWidth/minHeight styles
The 'people' icon on the right side DOES render correctly - compare why hamburger doesn't
critical_code_review_comments: Backend APIs are fully working - no issues found
Frontend hamburger button code appears correct but component not rendering
All menu page implementations reviewed and look correct (profile-edit.tsx, members.tsx, news.tsx, notifications.tsx, etc.)
SideMenu component exists and should work once hamburger button can trigger it

success_rate.frontend: 0% (hamburger not rendering, all menu tests blocked)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs all working. Frontend has critical bug where hamburger button TouchableOpacity is not rendering. I tried: 1) changing data-testid to testID, 2) adding minWidth/minHeight styles, 3) adding backgroundColor, 4) replacing Ionicons with plain text - none worked. The component exists in source but doesn't appear in DOM. Next agent should verify the fix renders the hamburger button first before testing side menu functionality.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Hamburger TouchableOpacity not rendering in React Native Web
investigation_done: Confirmed hamburger button JSX code is correct in home.tsx lines 155-161
Confirmed header uses flexDirection: row with space-between
Confirmed headerLeft has flex: 1 which should not collapse hamburger
Confirmed right-side 'people' icon TouchableOpacity DOES render
Tried multiple fixes: testID prop, minWidth/minHeight, backgroundColor, plain text icon
DOM inspection shows no element at hamburger position - component simply not in DOM
possible_causes: Something in the build/bundler stripping the component
A parent conditional rendering we missed
Expo Web specific rendering issue with the component tree
Gap styling or flexbox issue causing zero-size rendering
recommended_next_steps: Use React Developer Tools to inspect component tree
Add console.log in hamburger onPress to see if component mounts
Try wrapping hamburger in a View with explicit dimensions
Check if issue exists on iOS/Android native builds
